description: Reusable AI utility layer wrapping LLM provider APIs with usage tracking, BYOK, and billing. See docs/SHARED_AI_LAYER_DESIGN.md for full architecture.

userStories.title: Supabase migration: ai_usage_log and ai_models tables
userStories.description: Create Supabase migration file with ai_usage_log table (with all indexes) and ai_models table (with seed data for Claude Sonnet 4 and Haiku 4.5). See design doc Section 4.1 and 4.4 for exact schemas.
acceptance_criteria: Migration file exists in supabase/migrations directory
ai_usage_log table has all columns from design
Four indexes created on ai_usage_log table
ai_models table has all columns from design
Two seed model rows inserted successfully
Migration runs without errors on fresh database

userStories.title: TypeScript package scaffolding for @org/ai-core
userStories.description: Create packages/ai-core/ directory with package.json, tsconfig.json, src/ directory structure matching design doc Section 5.1. Export barrel file (index.ts). Add vitest config.
acceptance_criteria: packages/ai-core/package.json exists with correct dependencies
tsconfig.json configured for strict TypeScript mode
All source files created matching Section 5.1 structure
index.ts barrel file exports public API
vitest config exists and runs empty test
Package compiles without TypeScript errors

userStories.title: Define all TypeScript interfaces and types
userStories.description: Implement types.ts and errors.ts with all interfaces from design doc Section 5.2: AIClientConfig, AIClient, ChatParams, ChatResult, ChatChunk, GenerateParams, Message, Tool, UsageSummary, CreditBalance, DateRange. Create AI-specific error classes.
acceptance_criteria: All interfaces from Section 5.2 defined in types.ts
ChatChunk type supports streaming delta shape
Error classes extend base AIError with code field
Types are exported from index.ts barrel
No TypeScript compilation errors in types
JSDoc comments on all public interfaces

userStories.title: Implement model registry query in models.ts
userStories.description: Implement getModel() and getDefaultModel() functions that query ai_models table. Include cost calculation helper: calculateCost(model, tokensIn, tokensOut) → USD.
acceptance_criteria: getModel returns model by ID from database
getDefaultModel returns the is_default model row
calculateCost returns correct USD from token counts
Functions handle model-not-found with typed error
All functions accept SupabaseClient parameter
Unit tests pass for cost calculation math

userStories.title: Implement usage logging in usage.ts
userStories.description: Implement logUsage() function matching design doc Section 5.4 exactly. Insert row to ai_usage_log. Fire-and-forget pattern (don't await in hot path, but handle errors).
acceptance_criteria: logUsage inserts complete row to ai_usage_log
All columns from design doc Section 4.1 populated
Fire-and-forget: does not block calling code
Logging errors are caught and console.warned
Function signature matches design doc exactly
Unit test verifies insert payload shape

userStories.title: Implement basic chat() in client.ts with Anthropic SDK
userStories.description: Implement createAIClient factory and AIClient.chat() method. Call Anthropic messages.create(), log usage automatically, return ChatResult. Single provider only (Anthropic). No streaming yet. No BYOK — use env var key.
acceptance_criteria: createAIClient returns AIClient with chat method
chat() calls Anthropic SDK messages.create correctly
Usage logged automatically after successful call
Usage logged on error with success=false and error_code
ChatResult includes content tokens cost and latency
Managed API key read from environment variable

userStories.title: Implement SSE streaming via chatStream()
userStories.description: Add chatStream() method to AIClient. Returns AsyncIterable<ChatChunk>. Uses Anthropic SDK stream mode. Logs usage on stream completion with final token counts. Handle abort/error mid-stream.
acceptance_criteria: chatStream returns AsyncIterable of ChatChunk objects
Chunks contain delta text and partial token counts
Usage logged once on stream completion with totals
AbortController support cancels in-flight stream
Error mid-stream logs usage with success=false
Streaming response starts within reasonable latency

userStories.title: Implement retry with exponential backoff
userStories.description: Add retry logic to chat() and chatStream(). Retry on 429 (rate limit) and 5xx errors. Exponential backoff with jitter. Max 3 retries. Non-retryable errors (400, 401, 403) fail immediately.
acceptance_criteria: Retries on 429 and 5xx status codes only
Exponential backoff with jitter between retries
Maximum three retry attempts before final failure
400 and 401 and 403 errors fail immediately
Usage logged only on final attempt not retries
Unit tests verify retry behavior with mock SDK

userStories.title: Implement credit balance query and pre-check
userStories.description: In billing.ts: getRemainingCredits() queries ai_credit_balances for current period. checkCredits() estimates cost and compares to remaining balance. Pre-call integration: chat() checks credits before calling LLM when key_source is managed.
acceptance_criteria: getRemainingCredits returns current period balance
checkCredits estimates cost from model pricing table
InsufficientCreditsError thrown when balance too low
Credit check only runs for managed key users
BYOK users skip credit check entirely
Unit tests cover zero balance and sufficient balance

userStories.title: Phase 1 integration tests
userStories.description: Write integration tests that verify: createAIClient → chat() → usage logged, createAIClient → chatStream() → usage logged on complete, credit check blocks when balance is zero. Use Supabase test helpers or mocked client.
acceptance_criteria: Test: chat call logs usage row to database
Test: stream completion logs usage row to database
Test: zero balance blocks managed key call
Test: retry logic fires on 429 status code
All integration tests pass in CI environment
Test helpers documented for future test authors

userStories.title: Supabase migration: ai_api_keys table
userStories.description: Create migration for ai_api_keys table from design doc Section 4.2. Include unique constraint, indexes, and RLS policy (users see only their own keys).
acceptance_criteria: Migration creates ai_api_keys table with all columns
Unique constraint on user_id and provider enforced
Index on user_id and is_active exists
RLS policy restricts access to own records only
Migration runs without errors after Phase 1 migrations
Rollback migration drops table cleanly

userStories.title: Implement AES-256-GCM encryption module
userStories.description: In keys.ts: encrypt(plaintext, encryptionKey) and decrypt(ciphertext, encryptionKey) using Node crypto AES-256-GCM. Random IV per encryption. Encryption key from environment variable. Handle key derivation, IV prepend, auth tag.
acceptance_criteria: encrypt produces ciphertext with prepended IV and tag
decrypt recovers original plaintext from ciphertext
Random IV generated for each encryption call
Auth tag validated during decryption prevents tampering
InvalidKeyError thrown on wrong decryption key
Unit tests verify roundtrip and tamper detection

userStories.title: Implement key resolution with BYOK fallback
userStories.description: Implement resolveKey() matching design doc Section 5.3. Query ai_api_keys for active BYOK key, decrypt if found, fall back to managed env var. Update last_used_at on BYOK usage.
acceptance_criteria: BYOK key returned when active key exists for user
Managed key returned when no BYOK key found
Encrypted BYOK key decrypted before returning
last_used_at timestamp updated on BYOK key usage
Source field correctly set to byok or managed
Integration into chat and chatStream works seamlessly

userStories.title: Implement BYOK key management functions
userStories.description: Add saveKey(userId, provider, apiKey), deleteKey(userId, provider), validateKey(provider, apiKey) to keys.ts. saveKey encrypts and stores. validateKey makes a minimal API call to verify key works. Generate key_hint from last 4 chars.
acceptance_criteria: saveKey encrypts and inserts key to ai_api_keys
key_hint generated from last four characters of key
deleteKey deactivates key without deleting row
validateKey makes minimal API call to test key
Duplicate user-provider pair handled with upsert
Unit tests cover save validate and delete flows

userStories.title: Python ai_core package scaffolding
userStories.description: Create backend/ai_core/ with __init__.py, client.py, usage.py, keys.py, billing.py, models.py, types.py. Use Pydantic for types. Add pytest config.
acceptance_criteria: All Python module files created matching Section 6 structure
Pydantic models mirror TypeScript interfaces exactly
__init__.py exports create_ai_client function
pytest config exists and discovers test files
Type hints on all public functions
Module imports without errors

userStories.title: Python client.py with Anthropic SDK and usage logging
userStories.description: Implement create_ai_client() and AIClient.chat() in Python. Uses anthropic Python SDK. Automatic usage logging to same ai_usage_log table. Same key resolution pattern (BYOK → managed).
acceptance_criteria: create_ai_client returns AIClient with chat method
chat calls anthropic Python SDK messages.create
Usage logged to same ai_usage_log Supabase table
Key resolution follows same BYOK then managed pattern
ChatResult dataclass matches TypeScript ChatResult shape
Python unit tests pass for chat and usage logging

userStories.title: Python encryption and key management
userStories.description: Port AES-256-GCM encryption from TypeScript to Python using cryptography library. Same IV prepend, auth tag pattern. Port saveKey, deleteKey, validateKey.
acceptance_criteria: Python encrypt produces same format as TypeScript encrypt
Cross-language: TS-encrypted key decryptable in Python
Python key management mirrors TypeScript API exactly
cryptography library used for AES-256-GCM operations
Unit tests verify roundtrip and cross-language compat
validateKey calls Anthropic API with test request

userStories.title: Phase 2 integration tests
userStories.description: Test BYOK flow end-to-end: save key → chat uses BYOK → usage logged with key_source=byok. Test managed fallback when no BYOK key. Test Python-TypeScript encryption interop.
acceptance_criteria: Test: saved BYOK key used for subsequent chat calls
Test: managed fallback works when no BYOK key exists
Test: usage log shows correct key_source value
Test: Python decrypts key encrypted by TypeScript
Test: deactivated BYOK key triggers managed fallback
All Phase 2 integration tests pass alongside Phase 1

userStories.title: Supabase migration: ai_credit_balances table
userStories.description: Create migration for ai_credit_balances table from design doc Section 4.3. Include unique constraint, RLS policy, and trigger for updated_at.
acceptance_criteria: Migration creates ai_credit_balances with all columns
Unique constraint on user_id and period_start enforced
RLS policy restricts access to own records only
updated_at trigger fires on row modification
Migration runs after Phase 1 and 2 migrations cleanly
Default credits_remaining_usd is zero

userStories.title: Implement spending caps enforcement
userStories.description: In billing.ts: add spending cap check to pre-call flow. Check both user spending_cap_usd and admin_cap_usd. Lower of the two wins. Track credits_used_usd and deduct credits_remaining_usd after each call.
acceptance_criteria: Spending cap checked before each managed key call
Lower of user cap and admin cap enforced
SpendingCapExceededError thrown when cap reached
credits_used_usd incremented after successful call
credits_remaining_usd decremented after successful call
Unit tests cover both cap types and no-cap scenario

userStories.title: Stripe Checkout session for credit top-ups
userStories.description: Create Stripe Checkout session endpoint for purchasing AI credits. Support preset amounts ($5, $10, $25, $50). On successful payment, increment credits_remaining_usd.
acceptance_criteria: Checkout session created with correct amount and metadata
Success URL redirects back to app with session ID
Cancel URL returns user to credits page
Preset credit amounts of 5 10 25 50 supported
Stripe customer ID linked to Supabase user ID
Test mode checkout completes with Stripe test card

userStories.title: Stripe webhook handler for payment confirmation
userStories.description: Handle checkout.session.completed webhook. Verify Stripe signature. Credit user's ai_credit_balances. Idempotency: don't double-credit on webhook retry.
acceptance_criteria: Webhook verifies Stripe signature before processing
checkout.session.completed event credits user balance
Idempotency key prevents double crediting on retry
Invalid signature returns 400 status code
Unknown event types return 200 without processing
Integration test with Stripe CLI webhook forwarding

userStories.title: Phase 3 integration tests
userStories.description: Test full billing flow: purchase credits → chat deducts balance → spending cap blocks at limit. Test Stripe webhook idempotency.
acceptance_criteria: Test: credit purchase increases remaining balance
Test: chat call deducts cost from remaining balance
Test: spending cap blocks call when limit reached
Test: duplicate webhook does not double credit
Test: BYOK users unaffected by credit system
All Phase 3 tests pass alongside Phase 1 and 2

userStories.title: Add OpenAI provider support
userStories.description: Extend client.ts to support OpenAI via openai SDK. Provider resolved from model ID prefix or explicit param. Same usage logging, same key resolution. Add OpenAI models to ai_models seed data.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI chat completions called via official SDK
Provider auto-detected from model ID in registry
Usage logging works identically for OpenAI calls
BYOK key resolution supports openai provider
GPT-4o seed data added to ai_models migration
Unit tests cover OpenAI provider selection path

userStories.title: Implement org-level billing and admin controls
userStories.description: Add org_id support to credit balances and spending caps. Admin can set org-wide caps that override individual caps. Shared credit pool for org members.
acceptance_criteria: Org-level credit balance shared across org members
Admin cap overrides individual user spending caps
Org members see shared pool remaining credits
Admin can set per-user caps within org pool
Usage logged with org_id for org-level reporting
Unit tests cover org cap overriding user cap

userStories.title: Sonnet refactoring pass on Haiku scaffolding
userStories.description: Review all Haiku-generated code. Add: error handling edge cases, AbortController timeouts on all async ops, proper cleanup in error paths, consistent error messages, null safety on optional fields.
acceptance_criteria: All async operations have timeout via AbortController
Error paths clean up resources and close connections
Consistent error message format across all modules
Optional fields have null checks where accessed
No TypeScript strict mode warnings in any file
All existing tests still pass after refactoring
